MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Anthony E. Vieira, Esq. 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; Ashley E. Day, Esq. 408/961-4506 for deft & moving party NMC/Wollard, Inc.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling (GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE EXPOSURE. COURT AGREES THAT PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. SEE CHAKNOVA V. WILBER-ELLIS CO., (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 962, 975-976, FN. 12. HOWEVER, THE DRIVER DECLARATION DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PRODUCE ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT CONTROVERTING EVIDENCE OR DEMONSTRATE SUCCESSOR LIABILITY). Order signed in open court. (302/AJR/PB) Court Reporter Patricia M. Evezich, CSR #1193
